titlestmt.author: by Ellen Doon

PUBLIC "-//Yale University::Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library//TEXT (US::CtYBR::::[M. D. Ball Papers])//EN" "BALL.xml" converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 by v1to02.xsl (sy2003-10-15).

did.unittitle: M. D. Ball papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The papers consist primarily of scrapbooks containing newspaper clippings, manuscript notes, and Congressional bills regarding Alaska, but also include correspondence, customs documents, writings, maps, and a few photographs and printed illustrations. They document Ball's work as Customs Collector in Sitka at a time when that office was the chief representative of the U. S. government in Alaska; his term as Alaska's first delegate to Congress, 1882-85, and his campaign for representation and governance for the Territory; and other events in Alaskan history to 1887. Particularly well documented are the events of the winter of 1878-79, when U. S. citizens in Sitka appealed to a British warship for protection from native tribes. Ball's correspondence includes letters from Benjamin Harrison, William Dall, M. P. Deady, E. P. Lull, Marcus Baker, and John H. Mitchell, as well as from the Pacific Coast Steamship Company regarding his writing for their pamphlet "All About Alaska." The papers also include letters to Ball's daughter from James Wickersham, a 1927 blueprint for the Alaska flag, several printed and manuscript maps of Alaska, a manuscript map showing military operations of the Crimean War, and two photographs of St. Peter's by the Sea, a church in Sitka.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Edward Eberstadt and Sons on the William Robertson Coe Western Americana Fund, 1957.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The M. D. Ball Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: M. D. Ball Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Mottrom Dulany Ball (1835-1887) was born in Fairfax County, Virginia, and was educated at the College of William and Mary. He taught school until the outbreak of the Civil War, when he formed a cavalry company and rose to the rank of Colonel in the Confederate Army. After the war, he practiced law and founded the Virginia Sentinel , which folded in 1876. In January 1878, he was appointed Collector of Customs for Alaska, and moved to Sitka with his wife, Sallie Lewis Wright Ball, and children. In September of 1881, he was elected Alaska's first unofficial delegate to Congress. Ball served as U. S. District Attorney for Alaska from 1885 to 1887, and was the founding editor of the Alaskan , a Sitka newspaper.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The papers consist primarily of scrapbooks containing newspaper clippings, manuscript notes, and Congressional bills regarding Alaska, but also include correspondence, customs documents, writings, maps, and a few photographs and printed illustrations. They document Ball's work as Customs Collector in Sitka at a time when that office was the chief representative of the U. S. Government in Alaska; his term as Alaska's first delegate to Congress, 1882-1885, and his campaign for representation and governance for the Territory; and other events in Alaskan history to 1887.

Series I, Correspondence
, includes letters to Ball from Benjamin Harrison, William Dall, M. P. Deady, E. P. Lull, Marcus Baker of the USGS, and John H. Mitchell, as well as from the Pacific Steamship Company regarding his writing for their pamphlet "All About Alaska." Also present are letters to Ball's daughter from James Wickersham.

Series II, Papers
, is organized in two subseries, Documents and Writings. Documents include customs receipts from 1877 to 1879, petitions for Ball's election as delegate to Congress, printed bills and statements by Ball to Congress, a subscription for the 1880 4th of July celebration in Sitka, and a report on mining in Cook's Inlet in 1898 by B. Frank Hand. Writings consists of two draft chapters from a larger work about Alaska culture and history, a prospectus about travel in Alaska written for tourists, and a draft article about mining.

Series III, Scrapbooks
, consists of two bound scrapbooks and two loose pages from a third volume. The scrapbooks contain newspaper clippings, manuscript notes, congressional bills, and printed maps. The first volume, in Folder 21, largely concerns the events of the winter of 1878-1879, when U. S. citizens in Sitka appealed to a British warship for protection from native tribes, and the ensuing controversy.

Series IV, Visual Material
, consists of a 1927 Blueprint for the Alaska flag, several printed and manuscript maps of Alaska, a manuscript map showing military operations of the Crimean war, two photographs of St. Peter's by the SEa, a church in Sitka, and two lithographs removed from a Russian book, one of them showing the Sitka harbor.

subject.part: Indians of North America -- Alcohol use -- Alaska
Indians of North America -- Alaska -- Government relations

did.unittitle: Correspondence
TO M. D. BALL
Beardslee, Captain, U.S.S. Jamestown , Sitka
Brown, George, U.S.S. Alaska , Sitka
Butler, M. C., Washington, D.C.
Dall, William H., Washington, D.C.
Deady, Matthew P., Portland, Oregon
Goodall Perkins & Co. (Pacific Coast Steamship Company), San Francisco
Harrison, Benjamin, Washington, D.C.
Lull, Edward P., U.S.S. Wachusett , Sitka
Mitchell, John H., Washington, D.C.
United States Geological Survey, Marcus Baker
OTHER LETTERS
de St. Marceaux & Co., Reims, to Monsieur le Consul de France, Nouv. Arkhangel [Sitka]
Wickersham, James, Juneau, to Sallie Lewis Ball [daughter of MDB], Washington, D.C.
Papers
DOCUMENTS
Customs receipts and orders
Subscription for 4th of July celebration at Sitka
Petitions and ratifications of the election of MDB to represent Alaska at the 47th Congress
Printed bills, statements and reports by MDB to the 47th Congress
Typescript carbon, corrected, of report about mining in Cook's Inlet by B. Frank Hand, mining engineer
WRITINGS
"Illustrative Stories" and "Slavery and Witchcraft," manuscript chapters from a larger work
"Prospectus: The Great North West," manuscript and typescript
Article on mining, manuscript draft, corrected
Scrapbooks
Volume containing manuscript notes and clippings regarding Alaska's history, culture, and resources

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a detailed account (p. 67) of MDB's actions as Customs Collector 1878-79; many of the clippings concern Sitka's appeal to a British warship for protection from native tribes in February 1879, and its aftermath

did.unittitle: Loose clippings removed from pages 96-97 of above volume
Loose clippings removed from pages 96-97 of above volume
Loose clippings removed from pages 102-103 of above volume
Loose clippings removed from pages 102-103 of above volume
Bound volume titled "M. D. Ball/ Sketch-Book," containing clippings and maps

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes biographical article and portrait of MDB, 1882; clippings from the Alaska Herald 1874, Presbyterian Home Missionary [ca. 1885?] and Congressional Record for 1886; a census of the School Population of Southeastern Alaska 1885-86; other loose items; two maps tipped in at the back of the volume

did.unittitle: Two loose pages of clippings regarding the need for Territorial government and representation in Congress for Alaska
Visual Material
MAPS
Printed map of Alaska showing timber, tundra, and glaciers, from the tenth census of the United States
Manuscript map showing currents flowing down the West coast of North America (fragment)
Printed map, "Route of the Military Reconnaissance of 1883," with manuscript annotations by MDB
Printed map, "Alaska and Adjoining Region" [to the West]
Manuscript map, on cloth, of Tchitchagoff and Baranoff Islands and Chatham Strait, Alaska (fragment)
Manuscript map, on cloth, of Chatham Strait, Prince Frederick Sound, and nearby islands, Alaska (fragment)
Manuscript map of the Crimea, showing Sevastopol, Balaclava, and military positions in the area, ca. 1855
OTHER VISUAL MATERIAL
2 photographs of St. Peter's by the Sea, Sitka, one interior and one exterior view
2 lithographs from a Russian book, one showing Sitka's harbor, the other a portrait of Alexander [Taranov?]
Blueprint, "The Official Flag of Alaska, Adopted May 2, 1927," with swatch of blue silk
Oversize
Clippings removed from pages 96-97 of scrapbook in folder 21
Clippings removed from pages 102-103 of scrapbook in folder 21
Two maps of Chatham Strait, Baranoff Island, and nearby region
Map of the Crimea ca. 1855
Blueprint for Flag of Alaska
